title: CeTTa <-> MORK PathMap Bridge ABI (VarId/Symbol-Aware)
goal: Eliminate local rematch for trusted bridge rows while preserving exact CeTTa match semantics.
design_principles: Kernel comparisons are on ids, not strings.
Bindings are query-side semantic outputs, not candidate-side implementation leaks.
Packets must be self-describing and rejectable on contract mismatch.
Fast path is optional; correctness fallback is mandatory.
current_v1_issues: Rows can contain candidate-side keys that CeTTa cannot map soundly in chained nested-match flows.
Value payloads are text S-expressions, requiring parse/rewrite and increasing ambiguity.
Bridge index packet can under-approximate on complex variable patterns.

row_format.row_ref_count_u32: number of matched candidate atom indices

value_encoding.var_ref_semantics: references query var slots only; no candidate-side binders in output rows

validation_rules: If any row contains an unknown query var slot, reject packet.
If any row contains candidate-side binding keys, reject packet.
If any row fails binary decode, reject packet.
On rejection, fallback to native candidate + local rematch.

negative_examples.name: candidate_side_key_leak
negative_examples.reason: row tries to bind candidate var slot in output contract

negative_examples.name: text_payload_without_dictionary
negative_examples.reason: non-binary value payload in v2 packet

lane_split_tasks: MORK bridge lane: emit v2 packet with query-side-only binding rows and binary value encoding.
CeTTa lane: decode v2 packet to Bindings without parse/rewrite text path.
Conformance lane: lock nested-match and conjunction parity fixtures before re-enabling direct row fast path.

temporary_runtime_policy.cetta_pathmap_imported_query: native candidate + local rematch for semantic stability
temporary_runtime_policy.cetta_pathmap_imported_conjunction: default conjunction engine for semantic stability
